_sources.fact: Lee Industries, a major furniture manufacturer making sofas, loveseats, desks and tables, is headquartered in Conover with four plants in North Carolina employing about 700 people.

Hosiery mills historically produced pantyhose and socks in Conover; the Hosiery Technology Center was established in 1990 and is now known as the Manufacturing Solutions Center (MSC) as of 2012, part of Catawba Valley Community College.

Downtown First Avenue South underwent revitalization in the past five years with medians, decorative shrubs, bike routes, and businesses including old-style drug stores, boutiques, gourmet grocers, and antique shops. Major logistics operations including XPO Logistics and Cargo Integrated Logistics operate in Conover.

localH2: Built for Conover and the Catawba Valley.
localP1: Conover sits in the heart of the Catawba Valley as a working town built on furniture making and hosiery production. Lee Industries, one of the city's anchors, manufactures sofas, loveseats, desks and tables from its headquarters here, employing 700 people across four North Carolina plants. The Manufacturing Solutions Center, which grew out of the Hosiery Technology Center established in 1990, now serves manufacturers across the region from its downtown location, helping businesses improve quality and efficiency.
localP2: We build for the people who keep this town going: the furniture maker and logistics operator, the downtown boutique on First Avenue South, the manufacturer serving the Catawba Valley and beyond. Every site is designed and coded around your business, not stamped out of a template.
